props.caption: TSMC committed another $100bn to Arizona and named the agentic-AI market a driver of datacenter silicon — compute demand reaching for land, power and packaging.

items.note: TSMC’s additional US$100 billion Arizona buildout, alongside agentic-AI demand cited on its call, shifts the industry’s bottleneck toward land, power and advanced packaging.
The Spain–Argentina final on 19 July is a National Special Security Event led by the Secret Service. Wildfire smoke over the New York–New Jersey metro is forecast to clear before kickoff.
The UN identified gum arabic among the exports financing Sudan’s war economy. Before the conflict began in 2023, Sudan supplied roughly 70–80% of the world’s crude gum arabic.
A US 25% Section 301 tariff on certain Brazilian goods takes effect on 22 July. Brazil signalled possible use of its Reciprocity Law and opened a credit programme worth about R$13.3 billion.
Traffic through the strait remained about 91% below its pre-war baseline. Oil climbed roughly 4% on Friday as renewed US–Iran hostilities added another layer of supply risk.

items.what: Andy Burnham was elected UK Labour leader with backing from 379 Labour MPs and 23 affiliates. He is set to replace Keir Starmer as prime minister on Monday, completing a second Downing Street succession without a general election.

Eastern DRC’s Ebola outbreak reached 2,124 confirmed cases and 828 deaths through 15 July. The US barred recent visitors to the country from boarding US-bound flights, while seven Americans working on the response quarantined at a US-built facility in Kenya.

The European Commission proposed easing the EU emissions-trading scheme to support competitiveness. Its plan would slow the cap’s annual decline from 4.3% to 3.7% from 2031, extend heavy-industry free permits to 2038 and allow limited offsets.

Twenty-nine countries signed the founding agreement for a World Artificial Intelligence Cooperation Organization headquartered in Shanghai. At the same conference, Moonshot unveiled Kimi K3, an open model it describes as having 2.8 trillion parameters and a one-million-token context window.

Chile’s Vera Rubin Observatory began its ten-year Legacy Survey of Space and Time. Its 3,200-megapixel camera will capture a new image roughly every 40 seconds and survey the southern sky every three to four nights.

Strait of Hormuz traffic remained about 91% below its pre-war baseline, with one maritime-intelligence dashboard recording 26 transits through 16 July. Oil rose about 4% on Friday as US–Iran hostilities intensified again.
